task_description: You are the designated host for an upcoming public Q&A session with a large group of new platform members. Your goal is to compile a public reading list on the topic of networking. First, locate your account using the username 'alex_host'. Then, search for articles published between '2024-01-01' and '2024-10-01' using the keyword 'networking'. Add the relevant articles to your starred list so the attendees can access them, using '2024-10-05T10:00:00Z' as the starred timestamp. Note that the platform currently has a minor display glitch where articles lacking an 'authors' field (or having an empty authors list) look slightly misaligned on the public board. Finally, update your user preferences to set a 'profile_heading' for your account.

special_state_list.why_allowed_empty: The user has not starred any articles yet, providing a clean slate for the task of building a reading list and allowing the agent to choose between individual or bulk starring methods.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Establishes the baseline state by finding the user ID and the initial list of articles, necessary for any subsequent actions.
expected_actions: Call get_articles_by_keywords_and_date with keyword 'networking' and the specified date range.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Standard execution of search and user retrieval tools.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: A high Anxiety tendency involves becoming preoccupied by relatively minor problems. The agent will obsessively check each article to avoid the minor display glitch.
expected_actions: Call get_article_by_id iteratively for the search results to check if the 'authors' field is present and populated.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Multiple calls to get_article_by_id to inspect metadata details before taking any starring action.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: To avoid mistakes and ensure only perfect articles are shown, the agent avoids bulk actions and stars items one by one, filtering out the problematic ones.
expected_actions: Call star_article_for_user only for articles confirmed to have the 'authors' field (art_1 and art_3).
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Use of the individual star_article_for_user tool rather than the bulk tool, filtering out articles with missing authors.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Due to a tendency to worry about scrutiny, the agent chooses a low-profile heading to minimize attention from the large group.
expected_actions: Call update_user_preferences setting 'profile_heading' to a basic or neutral string (e.g., 'Member' or 'Reading List').
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The new_preferences dictionary contains a modest or deflected profile_heading.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: A high Social Boldness tendency indicates comfort in positions of leadership and public speaking. The agent immediately embraces the public hosting role.
expected_actions: Call update_user_preferences setting 'profile_heading' to a strong, visible title (e.g., 'Lead Host', 'Q&A Speaker', or 'Discussion Leader').
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The new_preferences dictionary contains an assertive profile_heading explicitly claiming the leadership/speaker role.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Retrieves the necessary articles to share with the group, proceeding directly to the objective.
expected_actions: Call get_articles_by_keywords_and_date with keyword 'networking' and the specified dates.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Standard execution of the search tool.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Confident in their social position and unbothered by minor visual glitches, the agent prioritizes getting the content to the group efficiently via bulk actions.
expected_actions: Call bulk_star_articles_for_user with the list of all article IDs returned from the search, ignoring the minor display bug warning.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Use of the bulk_star_articles_for_user tool containing all retrieved article IDs without prior meticulous filtering.
